raw_text:
Lead Product Manager
Prism Analytics — Transforming raw data into actionable product insights for modern teams.

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-First, US-based preferred)

Employment Type

Full-Time

Compensation

$190,000 - $240,000 USD annually

The Opportunity

We're seeking an experienced Lead Product Manager to oversee our core analytics platform and lead a distributed

team of 5-7 engineers. In this role, you'll own the product roadmap for our event ingestion and data modeling suite
—our highest-revenue product line. You'll drive strategic initiatives, mentor product thinking across the engineering
team, collaborate with Design and Sales, and directly impact how thousands of product teams worldwide make
decisions. This is a high-leverage leadership position for someone who thrives on autonomy, data-driven decision
making, and building high-performing teams.

About Prism Analytics

Prism Analytics is a B2B SaaS platform that helps product teams deeply understand user behavior and make data-

driven decisions at scale. Founded in 2018, we serve over 800 companies across fintech, SaaS, and e-commerce,
providing an integrated analytics and product intelligence layer that bridges engineering, design, and product

management. Our mission is to democratize advanced analytics so that every product team can compete with the
best, regardless of size.

Day-to-Day

Own the end-to-end product strategy for the event ingestion and data modeling platform, including roadmap
prioritization, feature validation, and go-to-market planning

Lead and mentor a team of 5-7 engineers, providing clear product vision, design feedback, and career

development opportunities while fostering a culture of ownership

Work closely with Engineering to scope technical initiatives, set realistic timelines, and balance velocity with

quality; establish OKRs and track product metrics using Amplitude and internal dashboards

Partner with Design to create user-centric experiences; conduct user interviews, validate hypotheses through
prototyping, and iterate on UI/UX for complex data workflows

Analyze product performance through SQL queries and Mixpanel cohort analysis; identify optimization
opportunities, bottlenecks, and expansion use cases

Represent product voice cross-functionally with Sales, Customer Success, and Strategy teams; develop
positioning, competitive differentiation, and pricing strategies

Champion a culture of experimentation by designing and running A/B tests, usage analytics investigations, and
feature adoption tracking

 Establish metrics frameworks and dashboards in Looker to track product health, adoption, and business impact;
present quarterly reviews to executive leadership

Manage escalated customer feedback and design solutions that address pain points while maintaining product

simplicity and developer experience

You Have

6+ years of product management experience, with at least 2+ years in a Lead PM or senior IC role managing
platform, infrastructure, or data products

Proven track record leading engineering teams (minimum 3-4 engineers) through product conception to launch;
experience with agile/scrum methodologies

Strong technical literacy: ability to write basic SQL queries, interpret query results, and communicate effectively

with engineers about technical trade-offs and architecture

Deep hands-on experience with analytics tools (Amplitude, Mixpanel, or Segment); proficiency in setting up
events, custom cohorts, and interpreting behavioral data

Demonstrated excellence in data-driven decision making: designing experiments, reading product metrics
dashboards, and using insights to drive prioritization

Excellent communication and leadership skills; ability to influence cross-functional teams, mentor junior PMs,

and articulate vision to both technical and non-technical audiences

Bonus

Experience building or shipping B2B SaaS products, especially in data, analytics, developer tools, or
infrastructure

Familiarity with Looker, Mode Analytics, or similar BI tools for dashboarding and self-service analytics

Background in SQL optimization, data warehousing concepts, or working closely with data engineering teams

Experience launching developer-focused products or managing APIs and SDKs

Prior experience hiring, scaling, or managing distributed/remote engineering teams

MBA or similar business education (not required but valued)

Our Stack

Amplitude (behavioral analytics and cohort analysis)   Mixpanel (funnel analysis and user segmentation)

SQL (query writing, data exploration, and validation)   Looker (business intelligence and dashboard creation)

Jira (agile project management and roadmap planning)   Figma (design collaboration and prototyping)

Segment (CDP and data integration)   Git/GitHub (for tracking technical docs and change management)

Python (for custom analytics scripts and data processing)   Kafka (understanding event streaming and infrastructure limits)

What We Offer

Competitive salary and stock options with quarterly refresh grants

Comprehensive health benefits including medical, dental, vision (100% premium coverage for employee)

 Unlimited PTO with a guaranteed minimum of 15 days per year plus 10 company holidays

Learning & development budget of $3,000 annually for courses, certifications, and conference attendance

Fully remote-friendly setup with $2,000 home office stipend and co-working budget

Parental leave (16 weeks paid for all new parents) and fertility/adoption support

Mental health support including subsidized therapy and meditation app subscriptions

Quarterly team offsites in San Francisco and annual all-hands in a fun destination

Apply

Apply directly through our careers page at prismaanalytics.com/careers or send your resume, cover letter, and a
one-page product strategy document (addressing how you'd approach the first 90 days in this role) to

careers@prismaanalytics.com. Referrals are encouraged and rewarded. We review applications on a rolling basis.

Familiarity with Looker, Mode Analytics, or similar BI tools for dashboarding and self-service analytics
Background in SQL optimization, data warehousing concepts, or working closely with data engineering teams
Experience launching developer-focused products or managing APIs and SDKs
Prior experience hiring, scaling, or managing distributed/remote engineering teams
MBA or similar business education (not required but valued)
